summary: Core PowerShell scripts show solid conventions (proper param blocks, error handling for missing files), but deliverable has critical gaps: (1) No Pester tests exist—task mandates TDD and `Invoke-Pester`-runnable tests, but DependencyLicenseChecker.Tests.ps1 is referenced but never provided. (2) No test fixtures or sample data. (3) Workflow installs PowerShell from scratch via apt/dpkg (wasteful—ubuntu-22.04 includes pwsh). (4) Circular test logic: workflow calls `Invoke-Pester` on nonexistent file; Test-Harness.ps1 runs `act` separately, then checks local files, not pipeline output. (5) act-result.txt requirement unclear—harness creates it but workflow doesn't. (6) Mock license lookup hardcoded in DependencyLicenseChecker.ps1 (line ~35), not configurable; requirements.txt parsing unimplemented (line ~93 comment admits it). (7) Weak assertions in Run-ActTest (checks only 'Job succeeded' string, not actual report contents). Violates explicit requirements: no TDD loop shown, no fixtures, incomplete manifest support. Positive: Run-LicenseCheck.ps1 has good error handling and output formatting; core compliance logic is sound.
